title: What Should Your First Credit Card Be?
author: Navees
summary: New to credit? Get the Discover Card. It's one of the easiest to get approved for, and it's how you start the journey.
body_html:
Your first credit card should be the Discover Card. It's one of the easiest to get approved for.

If you're a student, new to the country and just got your SSN, or an American in college starting your credit journey — get this as soon as you can. Establishing credit early is what lets you eventually qualify for the good rewards cards down the line.

Use it for 6 months, pay off the statement balance each month, and that means NO interest. Simple.

Denied by Discover? Get a secured credit card instead. Same idea: use it for six months, pay the full statement balance every month, and you'll build the history you need.

Start early. The boring beginner card today is what unlocks business class in a few years.
